results.evidence: delete/route.ts:47-48 calls updateSearchShard(searchKv, entry, 'remove') before db.$transaction at line 57. Test: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:135-143 and 145-153 verify call and ordering.
delete/route.ts:34 uses { id: true, ...SEARCH_ENTRY_SELECT } expanding from old 4-field select to all 17 SEARCH_ENTRY_SELECT fields. Test: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:166-187.
delete/route.ts:49 calls invalidateSearchIndex(searchKv) after updateSearchShard. Test: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:155-164 verifies ordering.
delete/route.ts:42-54 wraps KV ops in try/catch with console.error. DB $transaction runs unconditionally. Tests: delete/__tests__/route.test.ts:195-226.
search/route.ts:170-179 validates edge-only results via batch findMany IN query, filters non-existent. Test: search/__tests__/route.test.ts 'filters stale edge results against DB'.
search/route.ts:170 guard: searchSource === 'edge' && !pgSkipped. Postgres and edge+postgres sources skip validation. Tests verify mockSkillFindMany not called for both cases.
search/route.ts:180-182 catch block returns unfiltered results and logs warning. Test: 'returns all edge results unfiltered when DB validation fails'.
search-index.ts:276 passes { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS } to all kv.put in buildSearchIndex batch loop (name + author shards). Tests verify per-shard TTL.
search-index.ts:321 (name shards), :343 (author shard) pass { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS } in updateSearchShard. Tests verify both.
search-index.ts:286,356 pass { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS } to meta key puts. invalidateSearchIndex fallback kv.put also includes TTL. Tests verify all paths.

findings.title: invalidateSearchIndex fallback kv.put was missing expirationTtl
findings.description: The invalidateSearchIndex() fallback path (when kv.delete is unavailable) wrote empty meta via kv.put without expirationTtl, inconsistent with every other kv.put in the file. This empty meta marker would persist indefinitely instead of self-expiring.
findings.resolution: FIXED: Added { expirationTtl: SHARD_TTL_SECONDS } to the fallback kv.put call. Added test 'includes expirationTtl in fallback meta put' to search-index.test.ts.

findings.title: Existence validation fired unnecessarily when Postgres was deliberately skipped
findings.description: When edge returned 200+ results, Postgres was deliberately skipped (pgSkipped=true) for performance. But pgResults staying empty caused searchSource='edge', triggering a Postgres IN query for up to 200 names -- partially negating the pgSkipped optimization. The pgSkipped scenario means the system is healthy (edge has a full set), not that Postgres is down.
findings.resolution: FIXED: Added !pgSkipped guard to the existence validation condition. Added test 'skips validation when edge is full and postgres is deliberately skipped (pgSkipped)' to route.test.ts.
